assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item denominated in a foreign currency.
The question concerns hedge-accounting designation in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits recognised assets or liabilities to be designated as hedged items, and specifically allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements when the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets or liabilities as eligible hedged items at the top level, so a foreign-currency intragroup monetary receivable falls within the hedge-accounting model set potentially applicable to such exposures.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 establish a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign-exchange exposures linked to a net investment in a foreign operation, including qualifying monetary items within a group.
